So, point III is not correct.** ○ **Cold Currents are;** Somali Current and West Australian Current. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water on Earth Surface, Ocean Relief
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - Spring tides occur when the Sun, Earth and
Moon form a 900  angle relative to the Earth.
II. 	 During the neap tides, the sun and moon are
in a straight line.
III. 	 Tidal ranges are greatest when Earth is
farthest from the Sun.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Tides:** The periodical **rise and fall of the water level** in oceans and sea, once or twice a day, due to the gravitational pull of the sun and the moon, is called a **tide.**
● The three major forces that cause the occurrence of tides are as follows: ○ Moon's gravitational pull ○ Sun's gravitational pull. ○ Centrifugal force which acts opposite to the gravitational pull of the Earth.
● Tides occur due to an imbalance between the various forces acting on the ocean water at a point in time. The tide-generating force is the difference between these two forces; i.e. the **gravitational attraction** **due to the mass of the moon** and the centrifugal force due to rotation of the Earth.
● **Neap Tides:** After seven days of **spring tides the sun and the moon form a 90-degree angle** **between each other.** The resultant force of gravitation gives rise to a tide of very low magnitude
which is termed the neap tide. It also occurs twice in a month.
○ **Spring Tides:** When the position of the **sun, the moon and the earth** are aligned in such a way that it **forms a straight line,** the height of the tide will be higher than normal. These are called spring tides. It occurs twice in a month, once on the full moon and the other on the new moon. **So, statements I and II are not correct.**
● **Apogee:** When the **moon's orbit is farthest from the Earth,** it is called apogee. **Tidal ranges will** **be much less than average height during this period. So, statement III is not correct.**
○ **Perigee:** When the **moon's orbit is closest to the Earth**, it is called perigee. During this period, unusual heights of high and low tide occur.
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IPR, Institutions and Policies for IPR
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about the water vapour present in the atmosphere:
I. - The amount of water vapour decreases with altitude in the atmosphere.
II. 	 The amount of water vapour decreases from the equator towards the poles.
III. 	 The greater the concentration of water vapour in the air, the less dense the air is.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Water vapour** is water in gaseous form instead of liquid form. It is also the most important gas in the atmosphere. Some important facts about water vapour are as follows: The **Hydrosphere is the source** **of atmospheric water vapour.** It changes their state from vapour (gas) into liquid (water) and liquid into solid (ice).
● Water vapour is also a variable gas in the atmosphere, **which decreases with altitude.** In the **warm** **and wet tropics,** it may account for **four per cent of the air by volume**, while in the dry and cold areas of **desert and polar regions,** it may be **less than one per cent of the air.** Water vapour also **decreases from the equator towards the poles.** **So, statements I and II are correct.**
● The existence of water vapour also affects the atmospheric pressure. The **greater the concentration** **of water vapour in the air, the less dense the air is.** Air with lower water vapour makes it heavy or high in density. Because the molecular weight of water is less than the average molecular weight of dry air. Thus, dry air is heavier than moist air. Similarly, the air is lighter in the rainy season due to the presence of water vapour. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.** **Subject:** Geography | Climate Weather and Atmosphere **Tags:** Conceptual, Analytical

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText: With reference to world geography, which of the following correctly shows the relative locations of the Baltic Sea and the Dead Sea?

options.a: The Baltic Sea lies east of the Dead Sea
options.b: The Dead Sea is located west of the Baltic Sea
options.c: The Baltic Sea lies to the north of the Dead Sea
options.d: Both the Baltic Sea and the Dead Sea lie entirely within the European Plate

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Baltic Sea** is a marginal sea of the **Atlantic Ocean,** located in **Northern Europe.** It is considered the youngest sea on Earth, having formed around 10,000 to 15,000 years ago following the retreat of glaciers at the end of the last Ice Age. The sea stretches along a coastline of approximately 8,000 km and is bordered by multiple countries, including **Sweden, Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, Finland, Estonia,** **Germany, Denmark and Russia.** It is connected to the Atlantic Ocean through the Danish Straits, while the Kiel Canal links it to the North Sea's German Bight and the White Sea–Baltic Canal connects it to the White Sea.
● The **Dead Sea**, also known as the **Salt Sea,** is a saline lake situated in **Southwestern Asia,** lying between **Jordan** on the east and **Israel** on the west. Positioned east of the Mediterranean Sea and south of the Sea of Galilee, it is one of the most remarkable natural features of the region. The Dead Sea is renowned for being one of the saltiest water bodies on Earth, with a salinity of approximately 34.2%, making it nearly ten times saltier than average seawater.
● **The Baltic Sea lies to the north of the Dead Sea.**
● Dead Sea lies along the boundary of the African and Arabian Plates, while the Baltic Sea lies on the Eurasian Plate, hence, they do not both lie entirely within the European Plate.
**Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water in the Atmosphere (Clouds, Condensation, Precipitation, Rainfall, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to the thermocline in the ocean:
I. - It is the boundary layer where there is a rapid decrease in temperature with depth.
II. 	 Nearly 90% of the total volume of ocean water lies below the thermocline.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The temperature-depth profile for the ocean water shows how the temperature decreases with increasing depth. The profile shows a boundary region between the surface waters of the ocean and the deeper layers. The boundary usually begins around **100 - 400 m** **below the sea surface** and extends several hundred metres downward. It is a boundary region, **where there is a rapid** **decrease in temperature,** called the **thermocline.** **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● About **90 per cent of the total volume of water** is found below the thermocline in the deep ocean. In this zone, **temperatures** **approach 0° C. So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Ocean relief feature Characteristic
I. - Seamount Volcanic mountain rising from the ocean floor
II. - Submarine canyon Deep, steep-sided valley cut into the continental slope
III. Atoll Ring-shaped coral reef enclosing a lagoon
IV. Guyots Seamount with a flat top
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Seamount:** It is a **mountain with pointed summits**, rising from the seafloor that does **not reach** **the surface of the ocean.** Seamounts are volcanic in origin. These can be 3,000-4,500 m tall. The **Emperor seamount**, an extension of the Hawaiian Islands in the Pacific Ocean, is a good example.  **So,** **pair I is correctly matched.**
● **Submarine Canyon**: These are **deep valleys**, some comparable to the **Grand Canyon of the** **Colorado River**. They are sometimes **found cutting across the continental shelves and slopes,** often extending from the mouths of large rivers. The **Hudson Canyon** is the best known submarine canyon in the world.  **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Atoll:** These are low islands found in the tropical oceans **consisting of coral reefs** surrounding a central depression. It may be a **part of the sea (lagoon),** or sometimes form enclosing a body of fresh, brackish, or highly saline water.  **So, pair III is correctly matched.**
● **Guyots:** It is a **flat-topped seamount.** They show evidence of **gradual subsidence** through stages to become flat-topped submerged mountains. It is estimated that more than 10,000 seamounts and guyots exist in the Pacific Ocean alone. **So, pair IV is correctly matched.**
**So, all the four of the above pairs are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Distribution of Continents and Oceans
questions.questionText:
Which of the following factors affect the distribution of temperature of ocean water?
I. - Latitude
II. 	 Prevailing wind
III. 	 Ocean currents
IV. 	 Unequal distribution of land and water
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The temperature of the surface water of the oceans varies in much the same way as that of the land surface. This is because **insolation is responsible for the varying quantities of heat** which are received at different latitudes and in different seasons. Generally, the temperature is higher near the equator and gradually decreases towards the poles.
● The factors which affect the distribution of temperature of ocean water are: ○ **Latitude:** The temperature of surface water **decreases from the equator towards the** **poles** because the amount of insolation decreases poleward. **So, point I is correct.** ○ **Prevailing wind:** The winds blowing from the land towards the oceans drive warm surface water away from the coast, resulting in the upwelling of cold water from below. It results in the longitudinal variation in the temperature. Contrary to this, the **onshore winds pile up warm** **water near the coast** and this raises the temperature. **So, point II is correct.** ○ **Ocean currents:** Warm ocean currents raise the temperature in cold areas while the cold currents decrease the temperature in warm ocean areas. **Gulf Stream (warm current) raises** **the temperature near the eastern coast of North America** and the West Coast of Europe while the Labrador current (cold current) lowers the temperature near the north-east coast of North America. **So, point III is correct.** ○ **Unequal distribution of land and water:** The oceans in the **northern hemisphere receive** **more heat** due to their contact with a larger extent of land than the oceans in the southern hemisphere. **So, point IV is correct.**
● All these factors influence the temperature of the ocean currents locally. The enclosed seas in the low latitudes record relatively higher temperature than the open seas; whereas the enclosed seas in the high latitudes have lower temperature than the open seas. **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water on Earth Surface, Ocean Relief
questions.questionText:
Consider the following factors:
I. - Global wind patterns
II. 	 Earth’s rotation
III. 	 Distribution of continental landmasses
IV. 	 Tectonic plate boundaries
How many of the above forces cause the circulation of an ocean gyre?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● An **ocean gyre** is a **large system of circular ocean currents** formed by global wind patterns and forces created by Earth’s rotation. The movement of the world’s major ocean gyres helps drive the **“ocean conveyor belt.”** The ocean conveyor belt circulates ocean water around the entire planet. Also known as **thermohaline circulation**, the ocean conveyor belt is essential for regulating temperature, salinity and nutrient flow throughout the ocean.
● **Three forces cause the circulation of a gyre: global wind patterns, Earth’s rotation** and **Earth’s landmasses.** Wind drags on the ocean surface, causing water to move in the direction the wind is blowing. **So, points I, II and II are correct.**
● Earth’s rotation deflects, or changes the direction of, these wind-driven currents. This **deflection is a** **part of the Coriolis effect.** The Coriolis effect shifts surface currents by angles of about 45 degrees. In the Northern Hemisphere, ocean currents are deflected to the right, in a clockwise motion. In the Southern Hemisphere, ocean currents are pushed to the left, in a counterclockwise motion.
● Beneath surface currents of the gyre, the Coriolis effect results in what is called an **Ekman spiral.** **Earth’s continents and other landmasses** (such as islands) also influence the creation of ocean gyres. The massive South Pacific Gyre, for instance, includes hundreds of kilometers of open ocean.
● **Tectonic plate boundaries**, though important in seafloor spreading and ocean basin formation, **do** **not directly influence** the **surface circulation patterns** responsible for gyres. **So, point IV is not** **correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - An increase in the salinity of seawater increases the density of ocean water.
II. 	 Higher temperatures generally increase the density of ocean water.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● All waters in nature, whether rainwater or ocean water, contain dissolved mineral salts. **Salinity** is the term used to define the total content of dissolved salts in seawater. It is calculated as the amount of salt (in gm) dissolved in 1,000 gm (1 kg) of seawater. The density of sea water is **influenced by both its** **temperature and salinity**.
● **Increasing the salinity of seawater increases the density of the ocean water.** A higher salinity means more salt is dissolved in the water, increasing its mass without significantly increasing its volume, thus leading to a higher density. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Higher temperatures generally decrease the density of ocean water.** This is because warmer water expands, taking up more space and thus having a lower density per unit volume. **So, statement** **II is not correct.**
● **Cold and saline water are denser than warmer or less saline water** and therefore, they **tend** **to sink below the less dense water.** This phenomenon is a key driver of ocean circulation, known as thermohaline circulation. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water in the Atmosphere (Clouds, Condensation, Precipitation, Rainfall, etc.)
questions.questionText:
The major hot deserts of the world are located on the western margins of continents between 15° and 30° latitudes mainly because:
I. - influence of subtropical high-pressure belts throughout the year
II. 	 the rain bearing Trade Winds blow off-shore
III. 	 presence of cold ocean currents along the western coasts
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Deserts are regions of **scanty rainfall** which may be hot like the hot deserts of the Saharan type or cold deserts like ladakh.
● The major hot deserts of the world are located on the western coasts of continents between latitudes 15° and 30°N and S because they **are influenced by trade winds.** The **hot deserts lie astride the** **Horse Latitudes or the Sub-Tropical High Pressure Belts** where the air is descending, a condition least favourable for precipitation of any kind to take place. The **rain bearing Trade Winds blow off-** **shore** and the Westerlies that are on-shore blow outside the desert limits. **So, statements I and II** **are correct.**
● On the **western coasts of continents,** the **presence of cold ocean currents** cools the moist air moving in from the sea, leading to the formation of mists and fogs. However, this air becomes stable and dry due to the cooling effect, suppressing cloud formation and thereby limiting rainfall. As the air moves inland and comes into contact with the hot land surface, it warms up, but it remains too dry to produce precipitation. A striking example is the **Atacama Desert along the Chilean coast**, where the **cold** **Peruvian Current** creates such intense drying conditions that the average annual rainfall is as low as
1.3 cm, making it one of the driest places on Earth. **So, statement III is correct.**
● Whatever winds reach the deserts blow from cooler to warmer regions and their relative humidity is lowered, **making condensation almost impossible.** There is scarcely any cloud in the continuous blue sky. The relative humidity is extremely low, decreasing from 60 per cent in coastal districts to less than 30 per cent in the desert interiors. Under such conditions, every bit of moisture evaporates and the deserts are thus regions of permanent drought.
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Distribution of Continents and Oceans
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:** The Siberian type of climate is absent in the Southern Hemisphere.
**Statement II:** The southern continents are relatively narrow in the high latitudes. **Statement III:** There is strong oceanic influence in the Southern Hemisphere.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct and both of them explain Statement I
options.b: Both Statement II and Statement III are correct, but only one of them explains Statement I
options.c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement I
options.d: Neither Statement II nor Statement III is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Siberian climate** is an example of a severe continental climate and is characterized by very cold winters, relatively warm summers and extreme temperature variations between seasons. This type of climate occurs because of the vast landmass of Northern Asia (Siberia), which is far from the moderating influence of the oceans. **In the Southern Hemisphere, such a climate is absent because of the** **geography;** there **are no large, continuous landmasses in the higher latitudes like Siberia.** Instead, the Southern Hemisphere is dominated by oceans, which exert a strong moderating effect on the climate. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The **continents in the Southern Hemisphere**, such as South America and Africa, become much **narrower as they extend towards the poles.** Unlike the Northern Hemisphere, where massive landmasses like Siberia and Canada extend deep into high latitudes, the southern continents taper off and do not cover large, uninterrupted areas at these latitudes. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **Southern Hemisphere has vast oceanic expanses**, with fewer large landmasses interrupting them. Oceans heat and cool more slowly than land, so coastal and high-latitude areas **influenced by** **the ocean** experience milder winters and less extreme temperature variations than continental interiors. This maritime influence **keeps winters warmer and summers cooler**, preventing the harsh extremes typical of Siberian continental climates. **So, statement III is correct.**
● The **Siberian type of climate** is absent in the **Southern Hemisphere** primarily because the **southern continents are narrow at high latitudes,** which limits the development of extreme continental conditions. Additionally, the **strong oceanic influence in the Southern Hemisphere** **moderates temperatures,** especially in winter, preventing the severe cold typical of Siberian climates. Together, these factors explain why such a climate does not exist in the Southern Hemisphere. **So, both Statement–II and Statement–III are correct and both of them explain Statement–I.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Natural Vegetation (Forests, Trees, Vegetation)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
coniferous forests:
I. - Like equatorial rainforests, these have high
biodiversity and are dense with multilayered
vegetation.
II. 	 Unlike deciduous trees, these retain their
needle-like leaves for several years.
III. 	 Their soils are rich in nutrients and highly
fertile.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Coniferous forests,** also known as **taiga or boreal forests**, are typically found in high latitudes of the Northern Hemisphere. These forests are dominated by a few species of cone-bearing trees such as **pine,** **spruce and fir.**
● **Equatorial rainforests have high biodiversity and are dense with multilayered vegetation,** **whereas coniferous forests are more uniform and with moderate growth**. This is due to the colder climate and shorter growing season, which limits the variety and density of plant life. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● **Unlike deciduous trees, which shed all their leaves annually in autumn and grow new ones** **in spring, coniferous trees retain their needle-like leaves for several years**. These needles are adapted to withstand cold and dry conditions, reducing water loss through a waxy coating and small surface area. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Soils in coniferous forest regions are generally podzols, which are known for being heavily** **leached, nutrient-poor and acidic.** The cold climate **slows down the decomposition of organic** **matter**, while the accumulation of acidic needle litter from coniferous trees further contributes to soil acidity. Frequent rainfall in these regions enhances leaching, a process by which essential minerals are washed out of the soil. As a result, the upper soil layers are often low in fertility. **So, statement III is** **not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Climate Change and Impacts of Climate Change over Earth
questions.questionText:
The names Ladang, Taungya, Tamrai and Humah
refer to:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The names **Ladang, Taungya, Tamrai, and Humah are primarily associated with shifting** **cultivation practices in Southeast Asia**, including regions **like Java**, **Myanmar, Thailand and** **Indonesia**.
● These terms refer to traditional agricultural systems where **forest land is cleared using slash-and-** **burn methods**, cultivated for a few years and then left fallow to regain fertility. **Ladang in Malaysia** **and Humah in Java** and **Taungya refer to a system in Myanmar** that also integrates tree planting with crop cultivation. **Tamrai is used in Thailand** for similar upland shifting practices. These methods are mainly practiced by tribal and indigenous communities in forested and hilly areas.
● In India, this primitive form of cultivation is called ‘Bewar’ or ‘Dahiya’ in Madhya Pradesh, ‘Podu’ or ‘Penda’ in Andhra Pradesh, ‘Pama Dabi’ or ‘Koman’ or Bringa’ in Odisha, ‘Kumari’ in Western Ghats, ‘Valre’ or ‘Waltre’ in South-eastern Rajasthan, ‘Khil’ in the Himalayan belt, ‘Kuruwa’ in Jharkhand, ‘Jhumming’ in the North-eastern region, Pamlou in Manipur, Dipa in Bastar district of Chhattisgarh and in Andaman and Nicobar Islands. **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Distribution of Continents and Oceans
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Steppe climate, consider the
following statements:
I. - It lies in the westerly wind belt.
II. 	 This climate in the Northern Hemisphere
experiences extreme seasonal variations.
III. 	 Unlike the tropical savanna, it has sparse
grasses with very few trees.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A steppe is a dry, grassy plain. Steppes occur in **temperate climates,** which lie between the tropics and polar regions. Temperate regions have distinct seasonal temperature changes, with cold winters and warm summers.
● **These grasslands lie in the Westerly wind belt, which influences their general weather** **patterns.** However, due to their remoteness from oceans, they lack the moderating influence of maritime winds. As a result, even though they are in the zone of Westerlies, the Steppe regions remain dry and largely treeless**. So, statement I is correct.**
● While the **Steppe climate in the Northern Hemisphere**, especially in regions like Eurasia and North America, **experience extreme seasonal variations** - hot summers and very cold winters, this is **not true for the Southern Hemisphere**. In places like the Pampas of Argentina, which also fall under the Steppe climate zone, **the climate is more moderate with milder temperature** **differences** between seasons. This variation is due to differences in landmass and proximity to oceans in the Southern Hemisphere. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **The Steppe climate receives less rainfall than the tropical savanna,** which significantly affects the type of vegetation found in each region. In **Steppe regions, the limited moisture supports only** **short, sparse grasses with very few or no trees.** In contrast, tropical savannas, which receive more precipitation, support taller grasses and scattered trees. This difference in vegetation is one of the main distinguishing features between the two climate types. **So, statement III is correct.**
**Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Distribution of Continents and Oceans
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Sea Trade Route Connects
I. - Panama Canal Atlantic and Pacific Oceans
II. - Suez Canal Mediterranean Sea and the Red Sea
III. - Dardanelles Strait Aegean Sea and Mediterranean Sea
IV. - English Channel Atlantic Ocean and the North Sea
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Panama Canal:** It connects the **Atlantic Ocean in the** **east to the Pacific Ocean** in the west. It has been constructed across the **Panama Isthmus between** **Panama City and Colon by the United States of** **America (USA)** government which purchased 8 km of area on either side and named it the Canal ZoneIt. The canal connects nearly 2,000 ports in 170 countries and facilitated more than 14,000 transits in 2023. **So, pair I is correctly** **matched.**
● **Suez Canal:** The Suez Canal in Egypt connects the **Mediterranean with the Red Sea** and is a dividing line between Africa and Asia. The canal, which is at the opposite end of the Red Sea and the **Gulf of Aden's Bab Al Mandeb** **strait,** is the shortest maritime route from Europe to Asia and allows ships to avoid the long trip around South Africa's Cape of Good Hope. **So, pair II is correctly matched.**
● **Dardanelles Strait:** It is a narrow waterway forming the border between **Asian Turkey and European Turkey.** It is one of the narrowest water channels used to support international water navigation. It forms part of the border between Europe and Asia. The strait **connects the Aegean** **Sea to the Sea of Marmara,** allowing for passage to the Black Sea via the Bosporus Strait. **So, pair III is not correctly** **matched.**
● **English Channel:** It is the busiest ocean shipping lane in the world. **It connects the North Sea and the Atlantic** **Ocean.** More than 500 vessels go through it every single day to get from the North Sea to the Atlantic and from the United Kingdom to continental Europe – and vice versa. **So, pair IV is** **correctly matched.** **So, only three of the pairs given above  are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IPR, Institutions and Policies for IPR
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - Both Uranium and thorium cannot be found in the environment in their elemental forms.
II. 	 The uranium isotope, uranium-235 is the only naturally occurring fissile material.
III. 	 Uranium is used in nuclear reactors while thorium has been used in light bulb elements.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Uranium and thorium are naturally occurring, radioactive heavy metals with unusual properties. The energy generated by the natural breakdown of radioactive elements is immense and can be used in nuclear reactors.
● **Thorium is much more abundant than uranium** in rocks from the **Earth's crust;** it is found in small amounts in most rocks and soils. Uranium can form compounds with many metals; it reacts with hydrochloric and nitric acids, but other acids attack the element very slowly. Thorium is slowly attacked by water but, except for hydrochloric acid, does not dissolve readily in most common acids.
● **Uranium and thorium** are both silvery white-grey radioactive metals that corrode to black oxide in air. They are both malleable (can be pressed into shape) and ductile (can be beaten and drawn into a wire) and are very reactive and so **cannot be found in the environment in their elemental forms.** **So, statement I is correct.**
● The uranium isotope **uranium-235** is the **only naturally occurring fissile material.** This means it is able to undergo nuclear fission, a process used to create energy by the splitting of an atom's nucleus. **So, statement II is correct.**
● **Uranium** is used in **nuclear reactors to generate electrical power.** Electricity generation in nuclear reactors has many similarities to more conventional electricity generation in that the generated thermal energy is converted to mechanical energy that is used to drive turbo-generators (mostly by steam or superheated water).
● Thorium oxide (ThO2) has one of the highest melting points of all oxides and **has been used in light** **bulb elements,** arc-light lamps and welding electrodes as well as in heat-resistant ceramics. **So,** **statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. - Medical services for overseas patients in India
II. 	 Legal and financial advisors
III. 	 Government officials
IV. 	 Tourist guide
How many of the above are examples of quinary activities?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The highest level of decision makers or policy makers perform **quinary activities**. These are subtly different from the knowledge based industries that the quinary sector in general deals with.
● **Quinary activities** are services that focus on the **creation, re-arrangement** and interpretation of new and existing ideas; data interpretation and the use and evaluation of new technologies. Often referred to as **‘gold collar’ professions,** they represent another subdivision of the tertiary sector representing **special and highly paid skills of** **senior business executives, government officials,** **research scientists, financial and legal consultants, etc.** Their importance in the structure of advanced economies far outweighs their numbers. **So, points II and III are correct.**
● **Medical services for overseas patients in India** involves providing healthcare services such as **surgeries, diagnostics and treatments to international patients.** While it requires high skills and advanced infrastructure, it is classified as a **tertiary activity**, not quinary. **So, point I is not correct.**
● **A tourist guide,** though part of the service sector, does **not fall under the quinary category.** Their job involves providing information and assistance to tourists, usually drawing upon local or regional knowledge. This is more characteristic of **tertiary activities**, as it involves direct service delivery rather than policy-making, innovation, or expert data interpretation. **So, point IV is not correct.** **So, only two of the above are examples of quinary activities.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**
**Knowledge Box**
● **Primary activities** are directly dependent on the environment as these refer to utilisation of Earth’s resources such as land, water, vegetation, building materials and minerals. It, thus includes, **hunting and gathering, pastoral activities, fishing,** **forestry, agriculture and mining and quarrying.**
● **Secondary activities** involve the transformation of raw materials into finished or semi-finished goods, thereby adding value to natural resources. These activities form the backbone of the **manufacturing, processing and construction industries.**
● **Tertiary activities** encompass both the provision of services and the facilitation of exchange. On the production side, they involve offering intangible services such as **education, healthcare, banking and tourism** that are directly consumed by individuals or businesses. On the exchange side, they include essential support systems like **trade, transport and communication,** which help bridge distances and enable the smooth movement of goods, people, and information.
● **Quaternary activities involve** the collection, production and dissemination of information or even the production of information. Quaternary activities centre around **research, development** and may be seen as an advanced form of services involving specialised knowledge and technical skills.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Demographic Indicator Description
I. - Fertility Rate Number of live births per 1000 women aged 15–49 years
II. - Infant Mortality Rate Number of deaths of babies under one year per 1000 live births
III. - Maternal Mortality Rate Number of women who die due to childbirth per 1,00,000 live births
IV. - Sex Ratio Number of males per 1000 females in a given area
How many of the above pairs are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Most demographic concepts are expressed as rates or ratios – they involve two numbers. One of these numbers is the particular statistic that has been calculated for a specific geographical-administrative unit; the other number provides a standard for comparison. **The rate of natural increase** or the growth rate of population refers to the **difference between the birth rate and the death rate.**
● The **fertility rate** refers to the **number of live births per 1000 women in the child-bearing age** **group, usually taken to be 15 to 49 years.** **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● The **infant mortality rate** is the number of deaths of babies **before the age of one year per 1000** **live births.** Likewise, the **maternal mortality rate** is the number of women who die in childbirth **per 1,00,000 live births.** High rates of infant and maternal mortality are an unambiguous indicator of backwardness and poverty; development is accompanied by sharp falls in these rates as medical facilities and levels of education, awareness and prosperity increase. **So, pair II and III are correctly matched.**
○ Life expectancy refers to the estimated number of years that an average person is expected to survive. It is calculated on the basis of data on age-specific death rates in a given area over a period of time.
● The **sex ratio** refers to the **number of females per 1000 males** in a given area at a **specified time** **period.** Historically, all over the world it has been found that there are slightly more females than males in most countries. This is despite the fact that slightly more male babies are born than female ones; nature seems to produce roughly 943 to 952 female babies for every 1000 males. **So, pair IV is not** **correctly matched.** **So, only three of the pairs given above  are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: India and the World (USA, Russia, Middle East, Africa, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. - Argentina is the largest producer of platinum
in the world.
II. 	 India is the second-largest producer of Rare
Earth Elements after China.
III. 	 The Democratic Republic of Congo is the
leading global producer of cobalt.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **South Africa** is by far the **largest producer of platinum in the world**, with production amounting to an estimated 120 metric tons in 2024. Russia came in a distant second place, having produced some 18 metric tons that same year. Meanwhile, platinum mine production in the United States has experienced a decline in recent years, plummeting to about two metric tons in 2024. **So, statement I** **is not correct.**
● **China** has the **largest share of the world’s reserve of Rare Earth Elements (REEs)** – 44 million metric tons, with production of 2,70,000 metric tons annually. According to the United States Geological Survey (2025), China accounted for over one-third of the world’s total REE production – 3,90,000 metric tons. India has the **world’s fifth-largest rare earth reserves,** at 6.9 million metric tons, but there is no domestic magnet production. India relies on imported magnets, mainly from China. The monazite sands in Kerala are particularly rich in REEs. Monazite – a mineral mainly containing rare earths and thorium – is India’s principal source of rare earths. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● The **Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC)** is the world's **largest producer of cobalt** and holds more than 50 percent of the global cobalt reserves. Cobalt is used in lithium-ion batteries that form an integral part of electric automobiles, mobile phones and laptop computers. **So, statement III** **is correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. - Frequent natural disasters such as droughts
II. 	 Better employment opportunities
III. 	 Ethnic conflicts
IV. 	 Lack of access to basic healthcare and education
How many of the above are considered push factors for population migration?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Population migration** refers to the movement of people from one geographical location to another, either within a country (internal migration) or across national borders (international migration). Migration is often driven by a combination of **push and pull factors**. Push factors are those adverse conditions in a person’s current location that compel them to leave, whereas pull factors are favorable conditions in the destination area that attract migrants.
● **Frequent natural disasters like floods and droughts are examples of push factors for** **migration.** Natural disasters such as floods, droughts, earthquakes, or hurricanes cause immediate threats to life and property. They often destroy homes, agricultural lands, and infrastructure, making it difficult for affected populations to sustain their livelihoods. Consequently, people are compelled to leave their homes in search of safer and more stable environments. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Availability of better employment opportunities** in cities is not a push factor but **a pull factor.** Pull factors attract people towards a destination because they offer better prospects or quality of life. Employment opportunities in urban or industrialized areas draw migrants seeking higher incomes, career growth, or improved standards of living. **So, point II is not correct.**
● **Political instability, civil wars, ethnic conflicts** and other forms of armed violence are strong **push** **factors.** Such conditions create unsafe and unpredictable living environments, leading to displacement and forced migration**. So, point III is correct.**
● The **absence or poor quality of essential social services** like healthcare and education is a significant **push factor for migration.** When communities face inadequate healthcare, high disease burdens, or limited educational opportunities, families often relocate to improve their living conditions and prospects for their children. **So, point IV is correct.** **So, only three of the above are considered push factors for population migration.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Region often mentioned in the news
Country
I. - Essequibo Venezuela
II. - Abyei Ethiopia
III. - Tigray Sudan
IV. - Socotra Yemen
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Essequibo** is a disputed region that is **administered and controlled by Guyana**. Venezuela claims Essequibo as its territory and has taken recent political steps to assert this claim, but internationally and in practice, Essequibo remains under Guyana’s administration. **So, pair I is not correctly matched.**
● **Abyei** is a disputed region between **Sudan and South Sudan**. It is often in the news due to border tensions and peacekeeping operations. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● **Tigray** is a region in **northern Ethiopia**. It has been in the news due to the conflict between the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) and the Ethiopian government. **So, pair III is not correctly** **matched.**
● **Socotra** is an island in the Arabian Sea and is **part of Yemen**. It is in the news due to a humanitarian initiative launched by the UAE and the WHO to combat malnutrition among women and children. **So,** **pair IV is correctly matched.** **So, only one of the pairs given above is correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to the footloose industries:
I. - They do not require a large labour force.
II. 	 They are generally located near sources of raw materials.
III. 	 Diamonds and mobile manufacturing are examples of footloose industries.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Footloose industry** is a general term for an industry that can be **placed and located at any location** without effect from factors of production such as resources, land, labour, and capital. Following are some of the characteristics of the industry:
● These industries produce their products in small numbers and they **do not require a large labour** **force. So, statement I is correct.**
● Footloose industries produce in relatively small quantities, employing smaller workforces and are considered to be more efficient from an ecological point of view. These are generally non-polluting industries.
● **Footloose industries are not tied to any specific location or resources** and can be **established** **anywhere.** They depend on components and materials that can be sourced from various locations. **So,** **statement II is not correct.**
○ These industries can be placed and located at any location without effect from factors such as resources or transport. These industries often have spatially fixed costs, which means that the **costs of the products do not change despite where the product is assembled.**
● **Diamonds**, computer chips and **mobile manufacturing** are some examples of footloose industries. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
What is the correct sequence of occurrence of
the following cities in South-East Asia as one
proceeds from south to north?
I. - Bangkok
II. 	 Hanoi
III. 	 Vientiane
IV. 	 Phenom penh
Select the correct answer using the code given
below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Phnom Penh** is the capital of Cambodia and is located around **11.5° N latitude.** It is the southernmost among the four cities.
● **Bangkok** is the capital of Thailand and is situated at around **13.7° N latitude**.
● **Vientiane** is the capital of Laos and is located at approximately **17.9° N latitude**.
● **Hanoi** is the capital of Vietnam and lies at around **21.0° N latitude**.
**So, the  correct sequence of occurrence of the cities in South-East Asia as one proceeds from** **south to north is Phnom Penh - Bangkok - Vientiane - Hanoi.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**
**Relevance:** Recently, military clashes have erupted between Thailand and Cambodia.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
India is a founding member of the Eastern
Maritime Corridor (EMC), a sea route initiative,
which aims to connect:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Eastern Maritime Corridor (EMC):** The **Chennai-Vladivostok Sea Route**, also known as the EMC, connects **South India with Russia’s Far East region.** The corridor significantly reduces cargo transit time by up to 16 days and cuts distance by up to 40%, making it a highly efficient trade route.
● The EMC passes through key waterways, including the Sea of Japan, East China Sea, South China Sea, Malacca Straits, Andaman Sea and the Bay of Bengal.
● Ports along the route include Dalian, Shanghai, Hong Kong, Ho Chi Minh City, Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Bangkok, Dhaka, Colombo and Chennai.
● According to the recent data, a significant increase in cargo volumes on the EMC, with coal shipments rising by **87 per cent and crude oil transport** growing by 48 per cent year-on-year in **FY 2024–25.** The EMC is likely to see more volumes in the future, as the long-drawn Red Sea crisis forces many ships on the western sea route to avoid the Suez Canal and take the longer route via the Cape of Good Hope in South Africa.
**Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Insurance and Pension
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
River City located on the bank
I. - Danube Vienna
II. - Rio de la Plata Brasília
III. - Yangtze Shanghai
IV. - Seine Paris
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Danube River:** It is the second-longest river of the European continent, after the Volga River. The Danube River flows through **Vienna**, the capital of Austria. The river passes through 10 European nations of Germany, Austria, Hungary, Serbia, Bulgaria, Croatia, Slovakia, Moldova, Ukraine and Romania. It ultimately drains into the **Black Sea via the Danube Delta**. **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● **Brasília,** the capital of Brazil, is an inland city and is **not** located on the **Rio de la Plata.** Rio de la Plata is an estuary formed by the confluence of the Paraná and Uruguay rivers and lies between Argentina and Uruguay, far from Brasília. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● **Yangtze River:** It is the world’s third-longest river after the Nile and Amazon Rivers. With a length of 6,300 km, the Yangtze River is the longest in Asia and the People’s Republic of China.  It flows through 11 Chinese provinces, including Qinghai, Tibet, Sichuan, Yunnan, Chongqing, Hubei, Hunan, Jiangxi, Anhui, Jiangsu and **Shanghai. So, pair III is correctly matched.**
● **Seine River:** It is France s second-longest river after the Loire. This river has a drainage basin, known as the Paris Basin, of approximately 79,000 square kilometers and drains mainly northern France. It **flows** **through Paris** for about 13 kilometers from west to east. It finally **empties into the English** **Channel,** an arm of the Atlantic Ocean separating northern France and southern England. **So, pair IV** **is correctly matched.** **So, only three of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water in the Atmosphere (Clouds, Condensation, Precipitation, Rainfall, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to the Cool Temperate Western Margin Climate (British-type climate):
I. - It remains under the permanent influence of westerly winds throughout the year.
II. 	 It predominantly consists of deciduous forests.
III. 	 It experiences frequent occurrences of temperate cyclones.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Cool Temperate Western Margin Climate (British-type climate):** The cool temperate western margins are under the **permanent influence of the Westerlies all round the year.** They are **also** **regions of much cyclonic activity,** typical of Britain, and are thus said to experience the British type of climate. From Britain , the climatic belt stretches far inland into the lowlands of North-West Europe, including such regions as northern and western France, Belgium, the Netherlands, Denmark, western Norway and also northwestern Iberia. **So, statements I and III are correct.**
● There is so much oceanic influence on both the temperature and the precipitation that the climate is also referred to as the North-West European Maritime Climate. In North America, the high Rockies prevent the on-shore Westerlies from penetrating far inland and the British type of climate is confined mainly to the coastlands of British Columbia.
● The natural vegetation of this **climatic type is deciduous forest.** The trees shed their leaves in the cold season. This is an **adaptation for protecting themselves against the winter snow and frost.** Shedding begins in autumn, the ‘fall' season, during which the leaves fall and are scattered by the winds. The golden-brown leaves and the ‘naked’ branches present a very interesting scene. When they are in leaf the deciduous trees have **typically rounded out lines with thick trunks** and out spreading branches that yield valuable temperate hardwood. **So, statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Mountains and Plateaus
questions.questionText: The largest landlocked country in the world by area is:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Among the world's landlocked countries, **Kazakhstan is the largest,** covering an area of approximately **2,724,900 square kilometers**. It is followed by **Mongolia**, with an area of about **1,564,116 sq km** and **Chad,** which spans **1,284,000 sq km.** Other notable large landlocked nations include Niger (1,267,000 sq km), Mali (1,240,192 sq km) and Ethiopia (1,104,300 sq km). These countries, despite lacking direct access to the sea, possess vast land areas and diverse geographical features, often spanning deserts, plateaus and mountains.
**Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Distribution of Continents and Oceans
questions.questionText:
Consider the following description:
I. - It is found in subtropical latitudes between 30° - 40° latitudes.
II. 	 It is characterised by hot, dry summers and mild, rainy winters.
III. 	 It comes under the influence of sub tropical high in summer and westerly wind in winter. What is this type of climate?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Mediterranean climate** (classified as **Cs in the Koppen system**) is typically found along the western coasts of continents in the subtropical latitudes, roughly **between 30° and 40° north and** **south of the equator.** As the name implies, this climate is common around the Mediterranean Sea, but it is also found in regions such as Central California, Central Chile, southwestern and southeastern Australia and parts of South Africa.
● This climate type **experiences hot, dry summers and mild, rainy winters**. **During summer,** these regions fall under the **influence of subtropical high-pressure systems,** which suppress rainfall. While **during winter,** the **westerlies shift equatorward and bring cyclonic rainfall**. The average summer temperature hovers around 25°C, while winter temperatures typically fall below 10°C. Annual precipitation ranges between 35 cm and 90 cm, mostly concentrated in the winter months. This distinctive seasonal pattern makes the Mediterranean climate highly suitable for the cultivation of grapes, olives, citrus fruits and other drought-resistant crops.
**Therefore, option (a) is the correct answer.**

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. - Ethiopia
II. 	 Somalia
III. 	 Kenya
IV. 	 Eritrea
How many of the above countries are part of the Horn of Africa?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Horn of Africa:** It is the easternmost projection of the **African continent into the Arabian Sea.** Geopolitically and geographically significant, it comprises **Djibouti, Eritrea, Ethiopia and** **Somalia** and is sometimes referred to as the **Somali Peninsula. So, points I, II and IV are** **correct.**
● This region is notable for its diverse topography, ranging from the highlands of the Ethiopian Plateau to the Ogaden desert, as well as the coastal plains of Eritrea and Somalia. It is inhabited by a mosaic of ethnic groups, including the Amhara, Tigray, Oromo and Somali peoples.
● Its coastline is washed by the **Red Sea, the Gulf of Aden and the Indian Ocean,** making it strategically important for maritime trade routes. The region has had historic maritime and cultural links with the Arabian Peninsula and southwestern Asia.
● The **Bab el-Mandeb Strait,** located between Djibouti and Yemen, separates the Horn of Africa from the Arabian Peninsula. This strait is a vital chokepoint that connects the Red Sea with the Gulf of Aden and further, to the Indian Ocean, playing a crucial role in global shipping and trade.
● **Kenya,** while geographically close to the Horn of Africa and sharing a border with Somalia and Ethiopia, is **not considered** part of the Horn of Africa. **So, point III is not correct.** **So, only three of the above countries are part of the Horn of Africa.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.**
